factors.name: Fed Hawkish Hold & Rate Hike Signaling
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's debut FOMC held rates steady but signaled potential hike by September/October 2026 (50%+ hike odds per CME). New task forces on inflation frameworks removed forward guidance, creating uncertainty. Hawkish dot plot shift drove September hike probability from 30% to >50%, pressuring long-duration tech valuations.

factors.name: Real Yields Repricing Upward
factors.summary: Fed's hawkish stance without clear inflation framework triggered real yield expectations to rise. Long-duration growth stocks (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, TSLA) are highly sensitive to real rate moves; higher real yields compress multiples on unprofitable/high-growth mega-caps.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal & Oil Supply Relief
factors.summary: Trump signed US-Iran MoU, reopening Strait of Hormuz and easing geopolitical risk premium in oil. IEA forecasts supply glut in 2027; WTI retreated from prior highs. This reduces stagflation fears but also removes inflation upside that could have justified equities—net mixed.

factors.name: Apple Memory Chip Shortage & Price Hike Warning
factors.summary: CEO Tim Cook told WSJ Apple will raise prices due to memory chip crunch driven by AI capex demand. Morgan Stanley estimates 15% smartphone/PC price bump this year. This signals sticky goods-driven inflation impulse and margin pressure, complicating Fed's inflation read and potentially extending rate-hike pressure.

factors.name: Tech AI Capex Intensity & Semiconductor Bottleneck
factors.summary: Memory chip shortage is real and structural—hyperscalers competing aggressively for DRAM/NAND for AI training. Elevated capex costs and limited supply support inflation in computing infrastructure, reducing near-term FCF for mega-caps and extending duration pressure.

factors.name: Google Talent Drain (Noam Shazeer to OpenAI)
factors.summary: Key Google Gemini co-lead defects to OpenAI, signaling competitive AI talent loss at GOOGL. While single-name specific, reflects broader uncertainty in AI leadership hierarchy and potential execution risk for Google's AI roadmap.

factors.name: China Stimulus Weakness & Retail Collapse Signal
factors.summary: May China retail sales fell 0.6% YoY (first post-pandemic decline); PBOC rolled out third trade-in fund tranche but underlying demand remains soft. Weak China growth backdrop pressures MSFT, AAPL, and NVIDIA cloud/consumer exposure.

factors.name: Price Action Momentum - 24h Decline
factors.summary: NAS100 down 1.68% in 24h and -1.41% in 4h after FOMC. Move is already in progress, indicating Phase 2 extension of the hawkish Fed reaction. Early weakness but not yet severely extended.

factors.name: Fed Uncertainty & Dot Plot Divergence
factors.summary: Warsh removed forward guidance, creating binary risk around CPI/NFP releases. Market expectations now range from hold-all-year to December hike, widening volatility expectations. VXN likely elevated; dealer gamma in mega-cap names at risk if vol stays high.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness & Risk-On Sentiment (Transient)
factors.summary: Iran deal and peace optimism weakened USD vs. AUD, GBP, NZD intraday. Lower USD typically supports US equities, but this is being overwhelmed by real yield repricing. Risk-on sentiment is fragile and likely temporary given hawkish Fed tone.

screening.narrative: Kevin Warsh's debut FOMC delivered a hawkish hold with rate-hike signaling for later 2026, shocking markets and compressing long-duration growth multiples. The removal of forward guidance creates binary event risk around inflation prints, while Apple's memory-chip-driven price hike warning signals sticky goods inflation ahead. An Iran peace deal offered brief risk-on relief (weaker USD), but it was quickly eclipsed by real yield repricing higher, which is acutely painful for unprofitable/high-growth mega-caps like NVDA, TSLA, GOOGL, and MSFT. China's weak retail backdrop and structural chip shortages add to the growth headwinds. NAS100 is down 1.68% in 24h and early into a Phase 2 downside move; the catalyst (FOMC surprise) has hit, but the selloff is not yet exhausted.
catalysts_ahead: CPI release (likely early July) — critical for Fed re-pricing; any hot surprise risks further hawkish repricing
NFP / Unemployment data — labor market strength would support Fed hike narrative
NVIDIA earnings & forward guidance — hyperscaler capex commentary crucial for AI/chip demand outlook
Tesla earnings — growth and margin outlook amid macro weakness
Apple earnings — iPhone demand and supply chain pricing power
FOMC minutes (if released in window) — clarification on Warsh's inflation framework could ease or deepen uncertainty
screening.risk_warning: If CPI/PCE data comes in cooler than expected, or if Fed officials pivot dovish on Warsh's new framework, real yields could compress rapidly, triggering a strong snap-back rally in mega-cap tech. Similarly, if the Iran deal stabilizes oil and supply-side inflation fears ease without wage pressures, equities could re-price higher. Also watch for any mega-cap earnings beat/raise — single-name catalysts in MSFT, AAPL, NVDA, GOOGL can override macro for 1-3 days.
screening.entry_guidance: NAS100 move is Phase 2 (underway ~4h into FOMC reaction). Wait for retest of 29200–29400 support before fresh shorts; avoid chasing the decline from here. If support holds, look for bounces toward 29800 resistance as tactical longs into FOMC uncertainty.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Surprise (Warsh Dot Plot)
factors.summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's hawkish debut with more aggressive dot plot pushed markets to fully price a Fed hike by October (50%+ probability vs 30% prior). Higher real yields compress long-duration Nasdaq growth multiples directly.

factors.name: Real Yields & 10Y Treasury Repricing
factors.summary: 2-year Treasury yields surged post-FOMC as rate-hike expectations jumped. Risk assets sold off including equities; higher nominal and real yields are particularly painful for high-multiple mega-cap tech with long duration.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal & Oil Collapse
factors.summary: US-Iran ceasefire memorandum signed; Strait of Hormuz reopening; WTI crude collapsed to 3-month lows sub-$74 (>10% weekly decline). Lower energy costs could ease inflation narrative and reduce input cost concerns, offsetting some hawkish pressure, but no immediate bullish impulse for tech.

factors.name: Chip Sector Volatility Warning (JPMorgan)
factors.summary: JPMorgan warned of rising risk of market 'tantrums' as sharp swings in semiconductor stocks force investors to cut allocations. Semiconductor and AI-linked names (NVDA, ASML, etc.) are core Nasdaq 100 weight and vulnerable to forced de-risking.

factors.name: Intel Bounce on Apple Partnership Rumors
factors.summary: Trump announced Apple agreed to work with Intel on US chip production, sending Intel soaring. Positive for semiconductor narrative and could support Nasdaq-100 if sustained, but single-stock strength does not offset broader hawkish macro headwind.

factors.name: China Weakness & Stimulus Uncertainty
factors.summary: Chinese stock gauge nearing bear market on weak growth and tech slide; PBOC weaker yuan fix (6.8130 vs 6.7752 estimate) signals FX concern. Weak China reduces demand outlook for Nasdaq-100 multinationals with heavy Asia exposure (AAPL, MSFT, AMZN, etc.).

factors.name: European Macro Deterioration
factors.summary: Germany's Ifo institute cuts 2027 growth forecast on energy/tariff headwinds; SNB holds rates at 0% and warns of franc strength risks. Slower EU growth pressures multinational earnings and reduces global demand.

factors.name: BoE Hold Expected, Mixed UK Labour Data
factors.summary: UK unemployment fell to 4.9% (better than 5.0% expected) but wage growth firmed at +4.4% vs +4.0% expected. BoE likely to hold given geopolitical relief, but sticky wage data complicates 'lower inflation' narrative.

factors.name: Price Action: 4h -1.41%, 24h -1.68%
factors.summary: Nasdaq-100 down 1.68% in 24h from FOMC shock; 4h down 1.41%. Move is in early-to-intermediate phase; selling has accelerated post-Warsh but has not yet reached capitulation extremes or oversold levels on hourly charts.

factors.name: Prior Bias (2026-06-18 03:44 SELL6/10)
factors.summary: Previous read was SELL at 6/10 with no confirmation. Current hawkish FOMC + higher real yields constitute the confirmation trigger that was awaited; bias now strengthens but confidence remains modest due to move already underway.

key_levels.resistance: 29800–30000 (intraday supply from morning premarket highs); 30200 (recent swing high)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq-100 has rolled over sharply after Fed Chair Warsh's hawkish surprise, with the dot plot suggesting rate hikes remain live through 2026 and real yields spiking higher. Long-duration mega-cap tech is the primary victim of higher real rates, compressing growth multiples. While the US-Iran deal and collapsing oil prices are superficially risk-positive, they do not offset the structural headwind from Fed repricing and deteriorating China/Europe growth. Semiconductor stocks are flagged by JPMorgan as vulnerability point for forced allocations cuts. The 1.68% 24h decline is material but not yet capitulative.
catalysts_ahead: BoE rate decision (immininent, June 18 ~13:00 GMT) — hold expected; watch guidance for hawkish surprises
SNB decision (already concluded: 0% hold, readiness to intervene on CHF) — limited surprise risk remaining
US CPI/PCE data (next major inflation read, likely early July) — critical for Fed trajectory repricing
Mega-cap earnings (TSLA, NVDA, AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN guidance on AI capex and cloud demand)
Fed communications & dot plot interpretation over next 48-72h (Warsh's framework overhaul clarity)
Oil price stabilization or rebound (if Hormuz backlog clears faster than expected, inflation expectations could reset)
China stimulus announcements or weakness data (third trade-in fund tranche ongoing; retail sales weak)
screening.risk_warning: If BoE dovish surprise (cut signals) or major geopolitical de-escalation news lands, risk-on sentiment could snap back and send Nasdaq-100 rallying 1-2% intraday. If Fed speakers (next 72h) walk back Warsh's hawkish dot plot or cite inflation moderating faster, real yields could compress rapidly and re-ignite growth multiple revaluation. Oil rebound above $75 could rekindle stagflation fears and reverse the current selloff. Semiconductor positive catalysts (e.g., NVDA earnings beat) could also flip sector rotation. Current move is moderately extended (down 1.7% in 24h) — late entries face pullback risk.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (1-2h post-FOMC, decline underway but not exhausted). Prefer short entries on retest of 29800–30000 resistance or wait for a pullback to 29400–29600 (4h moving average zone) before adding shorts. If 29200 support breaks convincingly on heavy volume, Phase 3 acceleration lower could follow; otherwise, chop and consolidation likely through London/NY open.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Surprise (Warsh Debut)
factors.summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's debut press conference signaled inflation-fighting credibility; dot plot now projects one rate hike in 2026 (up from zero at March meeting), with some hawks penciling in two hikes. Markets pricing 44bp of tightening by Q2 2026. Long-duration tech multiples compress on higher real rate expectations.

factors.name: USD Strength & Real Yields
factors.summary: Dollar Index hits highest level since May 2025 on Fed hawkish shift; USD/JPY near two-year highs above 160.50. Strong USD headwind for NDX components with heavy international revenue; rising nominal yields and repricing of real rates pressure long-duration growth stocks.

factors.name: Treasury Yield Repricing
factors.summary: Front-end Treasury yields spiked after Warsh press conference; bond traders got a clear 'inflation hawk' message. Long-end yields rising despite initial selloff rebound—tightening bias caps growth multiple expansion, especially for mega-cap tech with duration sensitivity.

factors.name: Semiconductor Demand & Supply Tightness
factors.summary: Micron rallies on analyst commentary that memory-chip demand will outpace supply in near term; Apple facing ballooning chip costs (per MarketWatch). Demand outlook supports chipmakers (NVDA, AMD, QCOM) and AI capex narratives, partially offsetting macro headwind.

factors.name: AI Capex & Hyperscaler Momentum
factors.summary: Analyst raises Jabil stock price target to $460 on AI growth; Hive surges 10% on $220M Canada sovereign AI infrastructure deal. Supply-chain strength in AI infrastructure and semiconductor foundry space remains constructive for NDX, though geopolitical risk (Iran deal, crypto regulation) adds chop.

factors.name: Big Tech Buyback Squeeze
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports mega-cap tech AI spending spree is snuffing out steady share buybacks—one of key drivers supporting stock multiples. Reduced mechanical bid from buybacks in environment of margin pressure and capex surge headwinds NDX.

factors.name: Geopolitical Relief (US-Iran Deal)
factors.summary: Trump signed interim US-Iran MoU; Strait of Hormuz reopening, oil prices falling (gasoline sub-$4/gal), reducing inflation pressure. Short-term risk-on sentiment; energy stocks rally, reducing energy headwind to consumer-facing tech. Qatar restarting LNG exports.

factors.name: Price Action—Already Down 1.41% in 4h, 1.68% in 24h
factors.summary: NDX has already moved lower post-Fed hawkish surprise. Move is in early-to-mid Phase 2; further downside possible but chasing lower runs risk of extended move trap. Rally off Iran peace deal has been short-lived for equities (per FX commentary on EUR weakness).

factors.name: Antitrust & Regulatory Headwinds
factors.summary: DOJ probes US banks over Iran-related transactions; CME threatens to sue CFTC over Bitcoin perpetual futures approval. Yann LeCun (Godfather of AI) blasts Musk's xAI as 'failure,' casting doubt on AI startup valuations and indirectly meta-sentiment on tech bubbles.

factors.name: Bank of England Hold, Global Central Bank Divergence
factors.summary: BoE holds at 3.75% (7-2 vote); SNB holds at 0%; global central banks diverging from hawkish Fed. Suggests Fed is outlier, supporting USD and potentially creating carry unwinds in leveraged growth positioning.

screening.narrative: The Nasdaq 100 is caught in a Fed-induced selloff following Kevin Warsh's hawkish debut and dot plot surprise projecting near-term rate hikes. Real yields and USD strength are weighing on long-duration tech multiples despite constructive AI capex and semiconductor supply-chain narratives. The US-Iran peace deal has provided brief risk-on relief (oil down, equity futures up briefly), but equity follow-through has been muted—equities have already declined 1.68% in 24h, suggesting the hawkish Fed story dominates near-term price action. Bond traders have repriced faster than equity bulls have capitulated, creating a late-stage Phase 2 setup with risk of extended lower moves or consolidation chop into Friday's Juneteenth holiday and the next CPI/PCE cycle.
catalysts_ahead: Juneteenth holiday (June 19, Friday) — market holiday, reduced liquidity
Next CPI/PCE inflation print (exact date TBD in news window, likely late June) — could trigger major multi-factor repricing
NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL earnings (typically late June–early July for Q1/Q2 delivery) — guidance on AI capex will be critical retest of bull case
FOMC meeting minutes (late June likely) — will shed light on Warsh's actual stance vs. market read of hawkishness
China tech/chip news — potential escalation of chip export controls or Taiwan tensions could de-risk equities or spike VXN
screening.risk_warning: If Iran peace deal accelerates (60-day extension into final agreement), geopolitical risk premium collapses, oil falls further, inflation expectations reset lower, and Fed pivots back to dovish stance earlier than current dot plot suggests—this would flip bias to BULLISH and invalidate the hawkish rates thesis. Alternatively, if mega-cap earnings (NVDA, MSFT in particular) deliver blowout AI capex guidance and hyperscaler demand confirmation, sentiment could reverse sharply and NDX could bounce 2-3% into Juneteenth.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is SELL but move is already in Phase 2 (down 1.68% in 24h post-Fed). Wait for retest of 29900–30100 resistance or a stabilization/oversold bounce toward 29500 before considering fresh short entries. If NDX breaks below 29200 on volume, risk extends to 28800–28600 support (2-3% lower); avoid chasing lower unless technical confirmation (e.g. VXN spike, broken support, gap fill) emerges. Alternatively, use dips to support for long entries only if Fed hawkishness cools or next CPI/PCE surprise to the downside.
